calendar.matters:
Petition For Order To Compel Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-26-2017 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wendy Krog for petitioner Kimberly Naughton; Tim Watson for respondent John Anzalone.

In response to inquiry from the Court, both counsel confirm that there is no objection to the settlement going forward, and that they have nothing to add to the papers. The Court notes that the Notice of Hearing was not on the appropriate form. Mr. Watson acknowledged that he had received notice and wished for the hearing to proceed. The Court granted the petition, subject to review of the order. A hearing on another petition in the action set for June 26, 2017 was ordered off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition For Order To Compel Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order To Compel Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Machado for (trustee/respondent) John Anzalone.

Mr. Machado reported that they provided an accounting for the exemption trust in March and an accounting for the survivors trust from the date of death to the end of 2015, that they received objections to the exemption trust but no objections to the survivors trust and that he thinks that this petition can go off calendar and that they can work out the issues. The Court asked when the last communication from the Petitioner's was. Mr. Machado stated that his partner, Tim Watson, handles this case, that the last communication he is aware of are the objections and then a response addressing ancillary issues. The Court stated that without the appearance of counsel it will not take the matter off calendar. The Petition for Order to Compel Trust Accounting was continued to December 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and counsel is to notify the Court if this is no longer live and if there is no appearance on December 5, 2016 the petition will go off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order To Compel Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tim Watson for John Anzalone, Trustee/respondent.

Mr. Watson stated that the parties agreed to request a 90-day continuance of the hearing for exchange of information. Upon that representation, the matter was continued to September 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported

Petition For Order To Compel Trust Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wendy Krog for (petitioner) Kimberley Naughton. Timothy Watson for (respondent) John Anzalone.

The Court stated that it has the recent filings and asked if there is a way to agree on an accounting from 2010 forward. Ms. Krog stated that they have concerns, that they have been requesting accountings for a couple of years, that they have only been provided with spreadsheets and that it cannot be limited to 2010. Mr. Watson said that they are prepared to provide the accountings, that there are two accountings covering 4/14/10 to 12/15 when the exemption trust was funded, that one of the accountings should be ready soon and that they will provide the accounting and see if there are any objections. Ms. Krog stated that they have been trying to work with them but that delay is the issue. Mr. Watson stated that they need time to complete, that they need additional time to complete the survivor's trust back to 2010, that this is tax season for their accounting preparers, that they have had a difficult time obtaining records, that the surviving spouse would write checks and now they cannot get copies of those checks and that they need a 60 day continuance from 4/15/16. Ms. Krog stated that the trustee was ordered to provide an accounting from 2008, that they are entitled to it, that they realize that it will take time and that they have tried to work with counsel and are willing to give a reasonable amount of time. Mr. Watson stated that they do not have the records, that it is difficult to get them from Wells Fargo, that they only have some records for the survivors trust, that they do not believe that an accounting is required, that petitioners allege breaches, that they have provided information to show that the money is all there, that they suggest trying to provide an accounting for what they have or go to mediation and that they are ready to distribute. The Court stated confirmed that the time frame for an accounting going back to 4/14/10 is 60 days, that the petition to compel a trust accounting is continued to June 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that the accounting is to be done by then and that the next step is mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kinnear. (Not Reported)
